purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Approved requirement REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 and story ST-OBSERVATORY-CORE
Canonical project .sdlc records and existing CLI runtime conventions
outputs: Versioned observatory view-model normalizer with provenance and diagnostics
Read-only loopback HTTP API and static-asset server primitives
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
Node tests cover empty, partial, representative, legacy, malformed, traversal, symlink, invalid Host, and method rejection cases.
No normalized fact lacks provenance and no diagnostic leaks content outside the selected root.

notes: Use modular browser-native ES modules and code-native UI rather than adding a build tool.
Use browser QA only against the local loopback observatory.

contextualization.summary: Build the dependency-free, local-first normalization and HTTP core for Change Observatory. Canonical .sdlc evidence is authoritative; provenance must distinguish recorded, inferred, missing, and malformed data. The server is read-only and restricted to loopback.

context_sources.excerpt: id REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 kind requirement schema_version requirement v1 title Install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory summary Provide a local visual application shipped inside the Agentic SDLC plugin that reconstructs what was requested decided changed verified and released across stories phases contracts and iterations for technical and non-technical readers. status proposed acceptance_criteria After plugin installation agentic-sdlc observe --root <project> launches the included application without a separate build or cloud service. The overview answers What was asked? What changed? and Why was it decided? from canonical .sdlc evidence and links every claim to source records. The timeline shows story iterations across Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release distinguishing recorded inferred missing blocked and complete states. Contracts approvals decisions assumptions risks changed files tests gates handoffs sync events and release evidence are navigable with raw-record access. Natural-language explanations are clearly labeled as generated contain no private chain-of-thought and expose only stored rationale inputs outputs alternatives and evidence. T

id ST-OBSERVATORY-CORE title Normalize SDLC lineage and serve a safe read-only API schema_version 0.1.0 status draft phase implementation contract_id contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-CORE-implementation work_breakdown_id null acceptance Canonical KB records normalize into a stable versioned observatory view model with explicit recorded inferred missing and malformed provenance. Read-only loopback endpoints expose overview stories contracts changes verification and source records without path traversal or symlink escape. Empty partial legacy and malformed .sdlc states return bounded diagnostics and never invent historical facts. Core normalization and security invariants have deterministic Node tests. acceptance_criteria Canonical KB records normalize into a stable versioned observatory view model with explicit recorded inferred missing and malformed provenance. name agentic-sdlc-codex-plugin version 0.6.0 description Codex plugin for project contextualization verified assessments and traceable software delivery. type module bin agentic-sdlc ./bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs engines node >=18.18 files .codex-plugin/ assets/ bin/ docs/ lib/ schemas/ scripts/ skills/ templates/ scripts check node --check bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs && node --check scripts/generate-plugin-assets.mjs doctor node bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs doctor smoke node bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs --help test node --test keywords codex sdlc agentic knowledge-base author Antonio Antenore license MIT

# Architecture Agentic SDLC separates the reusable method from project-specific knowledge. ```text Codex plugin -> skill instructions -> templates -> schemas -> cross-platform CLI Target project -> .sdlc/ -> baseline -> assessments -> budgets -> contracts -> authorizations -> authorization-uses -> receipts -> capability-discovery -> output-contracts -> work-items -> work-breakdown -> dependencies -> stories -> orchestration -> locks -> handoffs -> decisions -> traces -> tests -> releases -> manifests -> archive -> cache -> indexes ``` ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph Plugin Reusable plugin Skill Skill instructions Templates Templates Schemas Schemas CLI Cross-platform CLI end subgraph Project Target project KB .sdlc source of truth Baseline Project baseline Assessments Assessment proposals and workflows Receipts Approval use generation verification and budget receipts Capabilities Capability discovery OutputRegistry Output contracts registry Breakdown Work breakdown agreements Dependencies Dependency graph Traces Append-only traces Manifests KB manifests Archive Archive plans Cache Local cache Indexes Search indexes end Skill --> CLI Templates --> CLI Schemas --> CLI CLI --> KB CLI

questions.question: What is authoritative?
questions.answer: Canonical records under the selected project .sdlc directory; caches and generated indexes are non-authoritative.

questions.question: May the model invent missing history?
questions.answer: No. Missing, malformed, legacy, and inferred states must be explicit and source-linked.

constraints: Use only Node.js built-ins and modular ES modules; do not add runtime dependencies or a build step.
Bind to 127.0.0.1 only, validate Host, disable CORS, apply restrictive CSP, and prevent traversal plus symlink escape.
All endpoints are read-only and bounded against oversized or malformed evidence.
assumptions: The existing .sdlc schemas remain heterogeneous across versions, so normalization must be tolerant and versioned.

approvals.summary: Freshness-only reapproval under Antonio's unchanged delegated Change Observatory scope after the story-to-contract reference was persisted; contracted behavior and exclusions are unchanged.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Freshness-only continuation of the already delegated Change Observatory delivery: reapprove the same three implementation contracts after refreshing their self-referential story context, then confirm the same three task starts; no material scope change and no install, external access, deployment, secrets, destructive actions, or unrelated work.
